name: auth0-mcp-server
description: Manage Auth0 applications, APIs, actions, logs, forms, and grants using natural language through MCP.
long_description:
Auth0 MCP Server is a local Model Context Protocol server for managing an Auth0 tenant with AI tools. It supports applications, resource servers, actions, logs, forms, and application grants, while preserving least-privilege controls through scoped Auth0 access and optional tool filtering.

This bundle can run with credentials already stored by `auth0-mcp-server init`, or you can provide `AUTH0_TOKEN` and `AUTH0_DOMAIN` directly through the host application's MCP Bundle configuration.

tools.name: auth0_list_applications
tools.description: List all applications in the Auth0 tenant or search by name

tools.name: auth0_get_application
tools.description: Get details about a specific Auth0 application

tools.name: auth0_create_application
tools.description: Create a new Auth0 application with the tenant. Prefer OIDC compliant unless otherwise specified.

tools.name: auth0_update_application
tools.description: Update an existing Auth0 application

tools.name: auth0_save_credentials_to_file
tools.description: Save Auth0 application credentials to the project's environment file. Only use this when you are in a project directory. Uses the framework quickstart spec to determine env variable names and target filename. Requires an explicit project path to prevent writing credentials to unintended locations. If the file already exists, conflicting keys are commented out and new credentials are appended (existing content is preserved). Additionally, a .gitignore entry is automatically added for the target file.

tools.name: auth0_list_resource_servers
tools.description: List all resource servers (APIs) in the Auth0 tenant

tools.name: auth0_get_resource_server
tools.description: Get details about a specific Auth0 resource server

tools.name: auth0_create_resource_server
tools.description: Create a new Auth0 resource server (API). Use RS256 for the signing_alg unless otherwise specified.

tools.name: auth0_update_resource_server
tools.description: Update an existing Auth0 resource server

tools.name: auth0_list_actions
tools.description: List all actions in the Auth0 tenant

tools.name: auth0_get_action
tools.description: Get details about a specific Auth0 action

tools.name: auth0_create_action
tools.description: Create a new Auth0 action

tools.name: auth0_update_action
tools.description: Update an existing Auth0 action

tools.name: auth0_deploy_action
tools.description: Deploy an Auth0 action

tools.name: auth0_list_logs
tools.description: List logs from the Auth0 tenant

tools.name: auth0_get_log
tools.description: Get a specific log entry by ID

tools.name: auth0_list_forms
tools.description: List all forms in the Auth0 tenant

tools.name: auth0_get_form
tools.description: Get details about a specific Auth0 form

tools.name: auth0_create_form
tools.description: Create a new Auth0 form

tools.name: auth0_update_form
tools.description: Update an existing Auth0 form

tools.name: auth0_create_application_grant
tools.description: Create a client grant that authorizes an Auth0 application to access a specific API with defined scopes. Required for machine-to-machine (M2M) communication using the client credentials flow. Use auth0_list_resource_servers to discover available APIs (audiences) and auth0_get_resource_server to look up available scopes before creating the grant.

auth0_token.title: Auth0 Management API Token
auth0_token.description: Optional access token for Auth0 Management API requests. If omitted, the server falls back to credentials stored by the init flow.

auth0_domain.title: Auth0 Tenant Domain
auth0_domain.description: Optional tenant domain such as your-tenant.auth0.com. If omitted, the server will try to infer it from a JWT access token.

read_only.title: Read-only mode
read_only.description: When enabled, only list and get operations are exposed. All create, update, and deploy tools are filtered out.

tools.title: Tool allowlist
tools.description: Comma-separated tool names or glob patterns to enable (e.g. auth0_list_*,auth0_get_*). Defaults to * (all tools).

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.description: Set to "false" to opt out of anonymized analytics collection.

debug.title: Debug namespace
debug.description: Set to "auth0-mcp" to enable detailed debug logging.
